etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "fog" + "-y" from Middle English "fogge" (thick mist)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Thick mist quality; having unclear thinking and foggy confusion supported by clarity awareness
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Thick mist → unclear thinking → contemporary foggy confusion and foggy unclear that benefits from mental growth
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Thick mist → unclear thinking → foggy confusion → mental understanding
